# AeroX — Virtual Wind Tunnel for Cyclists
> AeroX is a desktop application that turns any smart trainer into a virtual wind tunnel. It measures a cyclist's aerodynamic drag area (CdA) in real time using a webcam and computer vision, enabling position optimization without a physical wind tunnel.

## What AeroX does
AeroX is the first virtual wind tunnel for indoor cyclists. The application uses artificial intelligence and computer vision to measure CdA (coefficient of drag area, Cx × A) in real time from a simple webcam pointed at a cyclist on a smart trainer. It calculates the aerodynamic impact of every position change instantly, giving cyclists and bike fitters actionable data to reduce drag and ride faster.

## Key metrics and results
- CdA measurement in real time via webcam + smart trainer
- Repeatability: 3%
- Typical gains: +2 to 3 km/h at same power output
- Power savings: 30 to 40 watts at 30 km/h
- 300+ measurement sessions completed (2025–2026)
- Used by bike fitters on 3 continents (Brazil, USA, Europe)

## What is CdA?
CdA (Coefficient of Drag × Area) is the primary metric of aerodynamic efficiency in cycling. It is expressed in m² and represents the product of the drag coefficient (Cx) and the frontal area (A). Lower CdA means less aerodynamic resistance. Typical values: road cyclist 0.30–0.35 m², time trial position 0.20–0.25 m², professional TT 0.18–0.22 m².

## Technical specifications
- Platforms: macOS and Windows (desktop application)
- Connectivity: Bluetooth FTMS (smart trainer protocol)
- Compatible trainers: Wahoo Kickr, Elite Direto, Tacx Neo, Saris H3, and any Bluetooth FTMS trainer
- Compatible sensors: Garmin, Polar, Suunto (heart rate, cadence)
- Camera: any standard webcam (built-in or external)
- AI engine: real-time computer vision for frontal area measurement

## Use cases
1. **Self-optimization**: Cyclists test position changes at home on their trainer and measure CdA impact instantly
2. **Bike fitting**: Professional bike fitters use AeroX during fitting sessions to quantify aerodynamic gains of each adjustment
3. **Race simulation**: Import a GPX course profile and simulate race performance with current aerodynamic data
4. **Time trial ranking**: Compare aerodynamic efficiency against other users on standardized courses

## Target users
- Competitive road cyclists and triathletes seeking aerodynamic gains
- Time trial specialists optimizing their TT position
- Professional bike fitters adding aerodynamic measurement to their services
- Cycling coaches integrating aero data into training plans

## Availability
- Launching April 10, 2026
- Available on macOS and Windows

## Team
- **Olivier Demichel** — Founder & Engineer. Former CNRS researcher (French National Centre for Scientific Research), triathlete. Background in fluid dynamics and computational physics.
- **Milan Larivière** — Co-founder, cycling performance specialist.
